description: Fix 7 genuine defects found during a comprehensive platform audit: broken CI pipeline, a bug in adapter inspection, incorrect text decoding, a regex bug in Slack auth, missing IP address support in URL validation, a confusing error message, and dead ESLint config.

userStories.title: Remove non-existent script references from CI pipeline
userStories.description: As a contributor, I want the CI pipeline to pass so that pull requests get reliable feedback.
acceptanceCriteria: Line 28 of .github/workflows/ci.yml no longer references bun run build:plugins
Line 46 of .github/workflows/ci.yml no longer references bun run type-check:plugins
Line 131 of .github/workflows/ci.yml no longer references bun run build:plugins
All 7 CI jobs reference only scripts that exist in root package.json
userStories.notes:
File: .github/workflows/ci.yml

The Build job (line 28), Type Check job (line 46), and E2E Tests job (line 131) reference scripts that do not exist in root package.json: `build:plugins` and `type-check:plugins`.

Per CLAUDE.md: 'The root tooling (bun run build, bun run lint, etc.) does NOT cover plugins. When changing platform packages that plugins depend on, publish new versions to npm and update plugin dependencies.' Plugins are standalone projects with their own build pipelines.

The fix: remove these three lines entirely. Do NOT add new scripts — the architecture deliberately excludes plugins from root tooling.

- Remove `- run: bun run build:plugins` from the `build` job (after line 27)
- Remove `- run: bun run type-check:plugins` from the `type-check` job (after line 45)
- Remove `- run: bun run build:plugins` from the `e2e-tests` job (after line 130)

Do not skip E2E tests in acceptance criteria — they require a built extension and plugins, but the E2E fixtures handle plugin building internally via the test infrastructure.

userStories.title: Fix extension_check_adapter to return actual tool names instead of array indices
userStories.description: As a developer debugging adapter state, I want extension_check_adapter to return tool names so that I can verify which tools are registered.
acceptanceCriteria: handleExtensionCheckAdapter returns actual tool name strings (e.g., 'send_message') not array index strings ('0', '1')
The toolNames array contains the name property from each ToolDefinition in the tools array
toolCount still correctly reflects the number of tools

userStories.title: Fix getResourceContent base64 text decoding to handle non-ASCII correctly
userStories.description: As a user inspecting page resources, I want browser_get_resource_content to correctly display non-ASCII text (CJK, emoji, accented characters).
acceptanceCriteria: Base64-encoded text resources are decoded via Uint8Array + TextDecoder pattern, not bare atob()
The decoding pattern matches the established one in network-capture.ts lines 204-205

userStories.title: Fix Slack localStorage scan regex to include underscores in token character class
userStories.description: As a Slack plugin user, I want auth token extraction from localStorage to work correctly when tokens contain underscores.
acceptanceCriteria: The regex at the localStorage brute-force scan includes underscore in the character class: [a-zA-Z0-9_-]+
The regex matches the character class used in extractAuthFromScriptText at line 157
bun run build passes (run from plugins/slack/)
bun run type-check passes (run from plugins/slack/)
userStories.notes:
File: plugins/slack/src/slack-api.ts, line 248

Current code:
```
const tokenMatch = /(xoxc-[a-zA-Z0-9-]+)/.exec(raw);
```

The equivalent regex at line 157 correctly uses `[a-zA-Z0-9_-]+` (includes underscore). Slack xoxc tokens can contain underscores, so this regex would truncate the token at the first underscore.

The fix: Change `[a-zA-Z0-9-]+` to `[a-zA-Z0-9_-]+`.

IMPORTANT: This is a plugin file. Plugins are standalone projects excluded from root tooling. Run build and type-check from plugins/slack/ directory:
```
cd plugins/slack && bun run build
```
Do NOT run root bun run build/type-check/lint/knip/test for this story — they don't cover plugins.

userStories.title: Add IP address host support to URL pattern validation
userStories.description: As a plugin developer targeting a local dev server or internal tool at an IP address, I want my URL patterns like *://192.168.1.1/* to pass validation.
acceptanceCriteria: validateUrlPattern accepts patterns with IPv4 hosts like *://192.168.1.1/*
validateUrlPattern accepts patterns with IPv4 hosts with ports like *://192.168.1.1:8080/*
validateUrlPattern still rejects invalid patterns (existing tests pass)
Unit tests added for IP address host patterns in platform/shared/src/validation.test.ts

userStories.title: Improve validateUrlPattern error message to mention path requirement
userStories.description: As a plugin developer, I want the error message for invalid URL patterns to tell me that a path starting with / is required so I know to add a trailing slash.
acceptanceCriteria: The error message for patterns missing a path mentions the path requirement
The error message includes an example showing the correct format with a trailing slash
Existing tests that check the error message are updated if needed

userStories.title: Remove dead ESLint override for plugins/**/*.ts
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want the ESLint config to not contain dead overrides that could confuse future contributors.
acceptanceCriteria: The 'plugins/**/*.ts' entry is removed from the browser globals override files array in eslint.config.ts
No other references to plugins/ paths exist in eslint.config.ts overrides (the global ignore at line 41 already handles it)
userStories.notes:
File: eslint.config.ts, around line 128

The global ignores block (line 41) already excludes `plugins/**` from all ESLint processing. The browser-globals override at line 128 lists `plugins/**/*.ts` in its `files` array, but this will never match any file because ESLint skips `plugins/**` before reaching any override.

The fix: Remove the `'plugins/**/*.ts'` entry from the files array in the browser globals override block. The exact line depends on the surrounding context — look for the block that sets `globals: { ...globals.browser }` and has a `files` array containing browser extension and plugin paths.

This is a one-line removal. Do NOT restructure the ESLint config or make other changes.
